ops.title: Key-cache lazy-eviction momentary windows (Get/Put ABA during shutdown Close)
ops.description:
The key cache (internal/cache/cache.go) is a lazy, non-snapshot-isolated cache: there is an unavoidable sub-instruction window between a heal swap (or a Close reverting se) and the eviction/guard that follows. The gpt-5.6-sol round-2 review of v0.10.5 surfaced two such windows, distinct from the spared-forever ABA that v0.10.5 (evictStale(keep), cc-notes 42a9649) actually closed:

A) Get (cache.go ~380) can copy an identity-wrapped entry microseconds before the healing Put's evictStale runs; once copied, eviction cannot revoke the caller's local copy. If a Close has reverted se to memoryWrapper in that window, the guard e.wrapper != c.inner() passes and the entry is served.
B) Put (cache.go ~352) has a symmetric ABA: a degraded Put that captured memoryWrapper, paused, and resumed after heal->evict->Close can re-pass inner == c.inner() and insert an identity blob after eviction; conversely a genuine post-Close memory Put can be wrongly deleted by an earlier heal's delayed evictStale.

Why LOW / likely wontfix as-is:
- NOT introduced by v0.10.5 — both live in the Get/Put guards, which that change did not touch; they exist in v0.10.4 today.
- Reachable only when healingWrapper.Close interleaves. Close has exactly ONE caller: the daemon shutdown closer (daemon.go:169), which runs keyCache.EvictAll() immediately before it (daemon.go:168). So the precondition is a healing Put racing daemon teardown.
- The key VALUE served is always correct: heal changes only the at-rest wrapping (identity vs Enclave), not the derived Safe Storage key; dropping the Enclave key never invalidates an identity-wrapped (plaintext) entry. No wrong-key, no off-box leak (the plaintext is already resident in RAM). Worst case is one redundant Touch ID re-prime during shutdown.

If ever deemed worth closing (e.g. if Close becomes non-shutdown, like a live key rotation): record a monotonic wrapper generation on each entry, advance it on heal/Close, and have Put/Get validate it (eviction targets the captured pre-heal generation) — OR make Close terminal and reject all later cache ops. Both are larger changes to a working lazy cache and were consciously scoped out of the v0.10.5 patch under minimal-changes.
